value.cross_market_context: normal regular session; European equities steadied near the flatline after the prior session's tech-led selloff, with the STOXX 600 closing marginally higher while the major bourses finished mixed; idiosyncratic defence (Rheinmetall) and real estate (Segro) moves dominated single-stock action

text: European equity markets traded a normal regular session on Wednesday, June 24, 2026, and closed mixed after the prior session's tech-led selloff, with the pan-European STOXX 600 ending marginally higher. Live closing prints are evidenced by the LSEG FTSE 100 daily table (close as of 16:35 BST on volume of about 960 million shares) and dated June 24 closes across the Trading Economics Euro Area, Germany and France pages, plus the Reuters European close wrap. Daily moves are measured against the Tuesday, June 23 close. There is no cross-market calendar asymmetry: both European and US venues traded a normal session.

stoxx_europe_600.basis: Trading Economics dated EU600 table 635.16 / +0.08%; Reuters wrap closed 0.1% higher; MarketScreener widget 635.16, +0.08% (prior 634.63); corroborated across vendors

euro_stoxx_50.basis: Trading Economics EU50 table 6,219.46 / -0.18% (previous 6,230.55); Reuters/MarketScreener stocks-mentioned table 6,214.70 / -0.25%; CFD-tracked; both readings down about 0.2%

dax.basis: Trading Economics dated daily summary DE40 24,712.70 / -0.73% (previous 24,893.58); Reuters European cash close -0.6%; Trading Economics live CFD about 24,740 / -0.62% into the US session; disclosed basis discrepancy, magnitude bracketed -0.6% to -0.73%

cac_40.basis: Trading Economics FR40 8,385.49 / +0.54% (close about 8,385); snapped a three-session losing streak; corroborated by the Euro Area cross-index table

market_session: European regular close on 2026-06-24; FTSE 100 at the 16:35 BST London close

calculation.method: FTSE 100: June 24 close 10,461.63 minus June 23 close 10,428.85 equals +32.78 points (+0.31%); CAC 40: 8,385.49 minus 8,340.71 equals +44.78 (+0.54%); STOXX 600: 635.16 minus 634.64 equals +0.52 (about +0.08%); continental percentages are Trading Economics CFD-tracked day-changes corroborated by the Reuters wrap, except the DAX where Reuters cites -0.6% at the cash close versus the Trading Economics daily-summary -0.73%

text: European equities closed mixed on Wednesday, June 24, 2026, steadying after the prior session's tech-led selloff. The pan-European STOXX Europe 600 edged up 0.08% to 635.16 (Reuters: closed 0.1% higher). France's CAC 40 was the standout, rising 0.54% to 8,385.49 and snapping a three-session losing streak on a luxury rebound, and the UK's FTSE 100 added 0.31% to a precise 10,461.63. The EURO STOXX 50 eased 0.18% to 6,219.46 (Reuters/MarketScreener -0.25% to 6,214.70). Germany's DAX was the laggard and carries a disclosed basis discrepancy: the Trading Economics dated daily summary put the DE40 at 24,712.70, down 0.73% from 24,893.58, while the Reuters European cash close had the DAX down 0.6% and the Trading Economics live CFD sat near 24,740 (-0.62%) into the US session. Daily moves are measured against the June 23 close.

market_session: London regular session close, Wednesday 2026-06-24 (16:35 BST)

calculation.method: close 10,461.63 minus prior close 10,428.85 equals +32.78 points; +32.78 / 10,428.85 equals +0.31 percent

text: The FTSE 100 is the cycle's one precise official close and a relative outperformer: on the Investors Chronicle/LSEG historical daily table it opened 10,429.02, traded a 10,406.95 to 10,469.33 range, and closed 10,461.63 on volume of 960,305,976 shares, up 32.78 points (+0.31%) from Tuesday's 10,428.85. London outperformed as the continental benchmarks closed mixed, with Germany's DAX lower and France's CAC 40 higher.

subject.name: Muted, stabilizing session as Iran de-escalation lifts cyclicals while defence and commodities weigh

value.primary_support: investors assessed US-Iran de-escalation, easing energy prices and lifting consumer-discretionary and real estate names, offsetting weakness in defence, commodities and banks
triggers: US-Iran peace negotiations and improving Strait of Hormuz shipping
Germany scrapping a landmark F126 frigate programme, sinking Rheinmetall
awaited Micron earnings for chip-sector cues
value.rates: traders priced another 25 bps ECB rate hike by year-end per LSEG data
value.oil: Brent crude hit its lowest since the US-Israeli war on Iran began as supply-disruption concerns eased
value.macro: German Ifo business morale rose in June to its best current-situation reading in nearly two years

text: European shares were muted on Wednesday, steadying after the prior tech-led selloff as investors assessed US-Iran negotiations. Per Reuters, de-escalation and improving Strait of Hormuz shipping pushed Brent crude to its lowest since the US-Israeli war on Iran began, which weighed on energy and miners but eased rate pressure and lifted cyclicals. The dominant idiosyncratic event was Germany scrapping plans to build six F126 frigates, sending defence champion Rheinmetall to its biggest one-day drop on record and dragging aerospace and defence shares. German Ifo business morale rose in June to its best current-situation reading in nearly two years, and markets priced another 25 bps ECB hike by year-end. Investors awaited Micron's results for fresh chip-sector direction.

leaders.real_estate_note: Segro +17.4% after Prologis took its 16.6 billion dollar bid public; biggest intraday jump since March 2009
leaders.consumer_discretionary_note: luxury and retail rebounded - Adidas, LVMH, Hermes, Inditex up to 3%

laggards.commodity_note: tracking lower metals and oil prices
laggards.tech_note: reversed gains after the prior session's near-five-month-worst drop

text: The tape was a rotation out of defence, commodities and banks into real estate and consumer discretionary. Per Reuters, real estate rose 3% led by Segro's 17.4% surge after Prologis took its 16.6 billion dollar bid public (its biggest intraday jump since March 2009), while miners fell 2.5% and energy 2.3% on lower metals and oil, aerospace and defence lost 0.8%, industrials eased 0.2%, and tech reversed gains to fall 0.3% ahead of Micron. On Trading Economics data, consumer-discretionary names rebounded (Adidas, LVMH, Hermes, Inditex up to 3%) while banks fell as markets took profits. Sector percentages are carried at medium confidence; index-level closes are high confidence.

decliners.Rheinmetall_basis: Reuters -18.7% biggest one-day drop on record; Trading Economics component -14.79% to 996.60; intraday reports -13% to -19%
decliners.chips_note: Reuters cash close Infineon -1.2%, BE Semiconductor -1.3%, ASML -0.5%; Trading Economics later CFD had Infineon +1.29% and ASML -0.45% - cash-close vs CFD basis disclosed

text: The day's epicentre was Rheinmetall, which fell sharply - Reuters put it at -18.7%, its biggest one-day drop on record (Trading Economics component -14.79% to 996.60) - after Germany scrapped its F126 frigate programme; counterpart TKMS jumped 16%. The other standout was Segro, up 17.4% after Prologis took its 16.6 billion dollar bid public. Luxury and aerospace led the gainers: L'Oreal +2.83% (on a Jefferies target-price raise), Safran +2.20%, Air Liquide +1.99%, LVMH +1.92%, Hermes +1.50%, Inditex +1.12%, Kering about +1% and Airbus +0.95%. Decliners included TotalEnergies -1.84% on lower oil, Thales -1.2% on the defence drag, and Argenx about -7% on a drug-trial setback. Chipmakers were mixed by basis: Reuters cash close had Infineon -1.2%, BE Semiconductor -1.3% and ASML -0.5%, while the later Trading Economics CFD snapshot showed Infineon +1.29% and ASML -0.45%. Single-stock percentages are carried at medium confidence.
